hero.headline: What are the wage, overtime, and meal break laws in Massachusetts?
hero.subhead: $15.00/hr minimum. Overtime after 40 hours. One meal break. Triple damages if you get the timing wrong.

minimum_wage.question: What is the minimum wage in Massachusetts in 2026?
minimum_wage.answer_first: The Massachusetts minimum wage is $15.00 per hour, effective 1 January 2023 under M.G.L. c. 151, § 1. No increase is scheduled for 2026.

overtime.question: When does overtime apply in Massachusetts?
overtime.answer_first: Overtime in Massachusetts applies at 40 hours in a workweek, with a minimum rate of 1.5x the regular rate, under M.G.L. c. 151, § 1A. There is no daily overtime threshold.
overtime.exemption_note: These categories are exempt from state OT law (§ 1A) but remain covered by the federal FLSA.

meal_break.question: What meal break is required in Massachusetts?
meal_break.answer_first: Employers must provide a 30-minute meal break for any shift exceeding six hours, under M.G.L. c. 149, § 100.
meal_break.paid: Unpaid if employee fully relieved of duties and free to leave. Paid if employer requires on-site presence or duty.
meal_break.rest_break_federal_note: Federal FLSA treats 5-20 min rest breaks as paid working time. No MA state mandate for rest breaks.

sunday_holiday.question: Is Sunday or holiday premium pay required in Massachusetts?
sunday_holiday.answer_first: No. Sunday and holiday premium pay for retail workers was eliminated on 1 January 2023 as the final step of the 2018 Grand Bargain phase-out.

wage_act.question: What are the Massachusetts Wage Act penalties for employers?
wage_act.answer_first: Under M.G.L. c. 149, § 150, employers face mandatory treble damages (triple the unpaid amount) plus attorneys' fees and costs. Strict liability since the 2022 SJC ruling.
wage_act.accrued_vacation: Wages — must be paid on separation

faq_schema.q: What is the overtime threshold in Massachusetts?
faq_schema.a: Massachusetts requires overtime pay at 1.5 times the regular rate for all hours worked over 40 in a workweek, under M.G.L. c. 151, § 1A. There is no daily overtime threshold. Hotel, restaurant, and hospital workers are exempt from the state overtime law but remain covered by the federal FLSA.

faq_schema.q: Does Massachusetts require a meal break?
faq_schema.a: Yes. Under M.G.L. c. 149, § 100, no employee may be required to work more than six hours without a 30-minute meal break. The break is unpaid if the employee is fully relieved of duties and free to leave the workplace. If the employer requires the employee to remain on-site or on-call, the break must be paid.

faq_schema.q: Is Sunday premium pay required in Massachusetts?
faq_schema.a: No. Sunday and holiday premium pay for retail workers was eliminated on 1 January 2023 as the final step of the 2018 Grand Bargain phase-out. Standard overtime (1.5x after 40 hours in a week) still applies if Sunday hours push the total over 40 for that workweek.

faq_schema.q: What are the penalties for Wage Act violations in Massachusetts?
faq_schema.a: Under M.G.L. c. 149, § 150, employees who prevail in a Wage Act claim are awarded mandatory treble (triple) damages as liquidated damages, plus attorneys' fees and litigation costs. Since the Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court's 2022 ruling, this is a strict-liability standard: even one day of late payment triggers 3x damages, regardless of the employer's intent.

faq_schema.q: What is the minimum wage in Massachusetts in 2026?
faq_schema.a: The Massachusetts minimum wage is $15.00 per hour as of 1 January 2023, under M.G.L. c. 151, § 1. No increase is scheduled for 2026. The tipped minimum wage is $6.75 per hour, with tips required to bring total compensation to at least $15.00 per hour.

meta.title: Massachusetts Wage, Overtime and Meal Break Law 2026.Teamed
meta.description: Massachusetts minimum wage is $15.00/hr. Overtime at 40 hrs/week (1.5x). Mandatory 30-min meal break >6hr shifts. Treble damages for late pay under M.G.L. c. 149. EOR guide.
